text: Let's go live now to Capitol Hill where
the Senate has just taken the first step
to end the record-long government
shutdown. 40 days in counting.
>> This morning, our nation's airports
facing growing chaos and cancellations.
>> Tonight, millions of Americans who rely
on the government for assistance living
in limbo as the government shutdown
drags on. At this point, it's not clear
when and how much money will actually be
delivered to the nearly 42 million
Americans who rely on SNAP benefits.
>> The Trump administration has warned
Thanksgiving travel could be jeopardized
if the government remains closed.
>> Crazy headlines in America. The United
States government has officially been
shut down now for over 38 days, which is
the longest government shutdown in the
history of America. More than 1,000
flights have been cancelled in just a
single day because of this government
shutdown. At least 670,000
federal employees are on furlow. And
here's the other big one. 730,000
continue to work without pay. That
includes our military, our air traffic
controllers. So that is huge. Why does
the American government shut down like
this? basically because the Democrats
and the Republicans cannot agree on a
budget. And when they don't agree on the
budget, they can't continue operating as
normal. I don't care whether you're
Democratic or Republican. You're here to
serve the people and do what's best for
America. And I believe that this is not
best for America having a shutdown like
this. I cannot imagine going to work
every day, working hard, and not getting
paid for it. Food assistance programs
are stalled. It affects a lot of
different entities of America. Even if
another country, uh, South Korea for
instance, if you want to come and visit
America and you're looking to get a
visa, it's going to slow that process
down tremendously. to doing just trade
is going to slow that process down. Our
gross domestic product is going to
suffer as well. It affects a lot of
people financially. It affects every
spectrum you can imagine. When you have
a shutdown to this magnitude, the blame
game is over. Now is the time to fix
what's broken. I know it's easy for me
to sit here and say all that, but it's
just getting frustrating. and it gets to
a point where it's actually concerning
for our immediate future and our future
down the road. So, I really hope our
leaders do what's right for the American
people because that's why they're in
those positions. There's a lot of people
that rely and depend on government
programs, whether it be for food,
whether it be for housing, or or just
social security. Let's hope it winds
down quickly and we get the system
fixed. I'm really curious to know what
everyone else thinks about this
government shutdown, too. Let me know
what you guys think. Do you worry about
this in your own countries? Do you go
through this in your own countries? And
how do you deal with it? So, I'd love to
hear you chime in and give us your
thoughts. But for now, have a good night
or a good morning wherever you
